multiplot 0.3.3
  • Get started
  • Reference

Prism-style statistical comparison annotations

Source: R/stat_compare_means_prism.R
stat_compare_means_prism.Rd

Wraps stat_compare_means with defaults tuned to match GraphPad Prism's significance bracket formatting: P-value cutoffs at 0.05 / 0.01 / 0.001 / 0.0001, Prism-style label formatting, and bracket positioning appropriate for column / bar charts.

Usage

stat_compare_means_prism(
  comparisons,
  method = "t.test",
  p.format = c("prism", "signif", "numeric", "scientific"),
  label = c("bracket", "middle"),
  tip.length = 0.02,
  step.increase = 0.12,
  hide.ns = FALSE,
  ...
)

Arguments

comparisons

A list of length-2 vectors specifying the groups to compare (e.g. list(c("A","B"), c("B","C"))).

method

Statistical test method. One of "t.test", "wilcox.test", "anova", "kruskal.test". Default "t.test".

p.format

How to display p-values. "prism" (default) shows exact P value or "P < 0.0001"; "signif" shows significance stars only; "numeric" shows raw p-value; "scientific" uses scientific notation.

label

Position of the p-value label along the bracket. "bracket" (default) places the label above the bracket; "middle" places it on the bracket.

tip.length

Tip length of the comparison brackets in native units (default 0.02).

step.increase

Vertical spacing increment between stacked brackets (default 0.12).

hide.ns

If TRUE, hide "ns" (non-significant) annotations. Default FALSE.

...

Other arguments passed to stat_compare_means.

Value

A ggplot2 layer (invisibly).

Examples

if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{
library(ggplot2)
ggplot(ToothGrowth, aes(supp, len)) +
  geom_boxplot(aes(fill = supp)) +
  ggchoice("prism") +
  stat_compare_means_prism(comparisons = list(c("OJ", "VC")))
} # }
